mnemonics: Breadth-First Levels: Use 'BFL' to remember Breadth-First traversal processes nodes level by level, like reading lines in a book.
keyPoints: A binary tree is a hierarchical data structure where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left and right child.
Trees can be represented in memory using node structures with data, left child, and right child pointers.
Common traversals include Inorder (Left, Root, Right), Preorder (Root, Left, Right), and Postorder (Left, Right, Root).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the base case (e.g., NULL node) in recursive tree functions, leading to infinite recursion and stack overflow.
Assuming a binary tree is balanced; unbalanced trees can lead to inefficient operations with O(n) time complexity instead of O(log n).
